terms.definition: The normalized mean (0–1) of multiple domain-specific coherence scores, serving as an order parameter for societal stability.
A population held constant to isolate the causal effect of an independent variable—in this case, level of social constraint.
A quantitative measure of disorder or the number of accessible micro-states in a system; metaphorically, social fragmentation.
The theorized cognitive limit (~150) on stable interpersonal relationships an individual can maintain.
A bidirectional expectation that members of a community will aid one another, enhancing social capital.
Structural equivalence between two representations, enabling shared mental models across agents.
The unwritten communal code that specifies acceptable behavior and technology use within Amish districts.
In the Ising-model analogy, the normative pressure that biases individual states toward communal alignment.
A rapid systemic shift from one stable state to another—e.g., ordered to disordered—once a control parameter crosses a critical threshold.
A systemic property (e.g., authority, shared ritual) that couples individual agents into coherent collective behavior.
